response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict data handling standards for any organization processing EU residents' information, guaranteeing rights like data access, erasure, and explicit consent alongside a mandatory 72-hour breach reporting window. Non-compliance carries severe financial penalties of up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, resulting in landmark fines such as Meta’s €1.2 billion penalty in 2023 and Amazon’s €746 million fine. Despite criticisms over disproportionate compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and set a global legislative benchmark that has inspired similar data protection laws worldwide.
